subject: Sociology
title: Homeschooling and Alternative Education
description: A quick, practical guide to Homeschooling and Alternative Education.

hook.text: Millions of families pulled their kids from school to teach them at home. The reasons span the spectrum.

buildup.text: Homeschooling grew from religious conservatism but now attracts progressives, unschoolers, and safety seekers.

discovery.text: Alternative models like Montessori and democratic schools challenge the assumption that standard schools are best.

twist.text: Critics worry that homeschooling reduces exposure to diversity and lets parents control what children believe.

climax.text: The rise of alternatives reveals widespread dissatisfaction with mainstream education's one-size-fits-all approach.

punchline.text: The standard model isn't the only model.

quiz.question: Why has homeschooling grown beyond religious families?
options: Diverse groups are dissatisfied with standard schooling
